justification: All pass criteria met: (1) /health stub identified as 'cosmetic — returns 200 regardless of actual system health', (2) consequence clearly named — 'load balancers and orchestrators will route traffic to dead nodes', (3) specific dependency checks recommended — SendGrid reachability, DB connection pool, queue depth, (4) circuit breaker absence on SendGrid flagged as 'single biggest resilience gap', (5) verdict is NOT READY, (6) logging noted as 'adequate' coverage. All six pass criteria satisfied.

observations.text: Health check stub identified with exceptional clarity — called it 'cosmetic' and 'decorative', explained it 'lies' to orchestrators
Consequence articulated precisely: traffic routed to dead nodes, defeating the purpose of health checks entirely
Circuit breaker absence flagged as the single biggest resilience gap with detailed retry amplification analysis (3 retries × all requests = 4x traffic)
Specific health check recommendations: SendGrid reachability, DB connection pool, queue depth — exceeds rubric requirements
Verdict is clearly NOT READY with blocking items enumerated in priority order
Logging correctly noted as adequate coverage while identifying specific gaps in DB write failure logging
Goes well beyond rubric requirements — identifies slow response scenario, data integrity gap, idempotency concerns, dead-letter mechanism need, and missing on-call runbook
Minor: logging assessment is slightly mixed — says 'adequate' but then lists gaps including DB write failures and correlation IDs, which could be read as contradicting the 'adequate' assessment
